text.title: ArgoCD Configuration Patterns
text.summary: Repository structure with Kustomize, RBAC via Projects, sync waves, hooks, notifications, and sync windows.

rule.statement: Separate the GitOps config repository from the application source code repository — never mix Dockerfiles and Kubernetes manifests in the same repo.
rule.rationale: Application CI pushes image tag updates to the config repo. If they share a repo, the image build triggers another pipeline that triggers another build — infinite loop.

example.code: kubernetes-manifests/ # GitOps repo ├── apps/ │ └── my-app/ │ ├── base/ │ │ ├── kustomization.yaml │ │ ├── deployment.yaml │ │ └── service.yaml │ └── overlays/ │ ├── staging/ # patches: image tag, replicas │ └── production/ # patches: image tag, replicas, HPA ├── infrastructure/ │ ├── cert-manager/ │ └── external-secrets/ └── argocd/ ├── projects/ └── applicationsets/

section.title: RBAC via Projects

rule.statement: Create an AppProject per team with explicit sourceRepos, destinations, and roles — do not deploy all applications to the default project.
rule.rationale: The default project has no restrictions. An AppProject scopes a team to their repos and namespaces, preventing accidental or malicious cross-team deployments.

section.reference: AppProject with RBAC for a team

rule.statement: Use sync-wave annotations to order resource creation within a sync: namespaces and CRDs at wave -1, ConfigMaps and Secrets at wave 0, Deployments at wave 1, Ingress at wave 2.
rule.rationale: Without sync waves, ArgoCD applies all resources simultaneously. A Deployment that depends on a CRD fails if the CRD has not been installed yet.

example.code: # Namespace — created first metadata: annotations: argocd.argoproj.io/sync-wave: "-1" --- # Database migration Job — before app starts metadata: annotations: argocd.argoproj.io/hook: PreSync argocd.argoproj.io/hook-delete-policy: HookSucceeded

rule.statement: Configure notifications for on-sync-failed and on-health-degraded triggers at minimum; send to both Slack and a ticketing system for production.
rule.rationale: Sync failures without notification go undetected. ArgoCD self-heals configuration drift but cannot fix broken images or missing dependencies — human alert is required.

list.item: Required triggers: on-sync-failed, on-health-degraded, on-deployed (for production audit trail)
Optional: on-sync-running (noisy for high-velocity teams), on-sync-succeeded
Notification channels: Slack for operational, GitHub/GitLab status for PR gates, email for compliance
